summary: Jeff Atwood explains how to extend the Windows Vista evaluation period from 30 to 120 days using Microsoft's official slmgr utility. The post walks through the command-line process, discusses OEM pricing for different Vista editions, and provides technical details about the licensing management tool. He notes this is a legitimate, supported feature rather than a hack, and shares his personal experience using it to evaluate Vista Ultimate before committing to purchase.
key_points: Every Vista DVD allows installation without a product key, giving a 30-day evaluation period that can be extended to 120 days using the slmgr -rearm command
OEM Vista pricing ranges from $100 for Home Basic to $200 for Ultimate, with little savings over retail upgrades at the low end
The Vista Family Pack offers a discount option with two $49 copies of Home Premium when purchasing Ultimate at full price
The slmgr.vbs script provides various licensing management functions including activation, license display, and product key management
Enterprise edition uses a different activation scheme with a local volume license key server and only allows a 3-day grace period
money_quotes: If you're on the fence about the impending release of Windows Vista, I recommend trying before you buy.
Every Vista DVD includes the ability to install any edition of Vista without a product key.
You can easily extend the 30-day Windows Vista grace period to 120 days. No hacks required. This is an official, supported operation directly from Microsoft.
At the end of that 120 day grace period, you'll have to pony up a license fee for the edition of Vista you've installed.
Microsoft is semi-officially supporting the OEM versions for resale to end users, but the bad news is that there's not much savings at the low-end.
I now have 29 days to decide if I want Ultimate edition or not.
key_insight: Microsoft provides an official, supported way to extend your Vista evaluation to 120 days using the slmgr -rearm command, giving users ample time to make an informed purchasing decision.
